description_long: This object is a rustic, hand-woven wicker basket or barrel with a wide, squat body and a narrow, slightly flared neck. The exterior is composed of interlaced natural fibers, giving it a textured, organic appearance with visible horizontal and vertical weaving patterns. The rim is reinforced and appears darker, possibly from wear or a different material, while the body shows subtle variations in color, suggesting age or exposure to the elements. The opening at the top is circular and unobstructed, indicating it is designed to hold or carry items. There are no visible handles or lids, and the structure is slightly asymmetrical, adding to its handmade character. This type of object would commonly be found in outdoor or garden settings, used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A hand-woven, rustic wicker barrel or basket with a wide body, narrow neck, and textured, natural finish. The opening is circular and rimmed, with no handles or lid present.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, rounded body and the circular, reinforced rim of the opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated, slightly tapered profile and the woven texture along the length.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical woven pattern and the continuous curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the narrowing neck and the consistent weave structure.
